commentary_text: The sportsbook consensus assigns England a 81% win probability versus the model's 63%, a 18-percentage-point gap favoring the favorite. The market is also notably more pessimistic on Panama, pricing a 6.5% win chance against the model's 11.9%. This sharper conviction on England could reflect recent lineup developments, injury status, or form trends that the model has not yet incorporated; conversely, it may simply reflect real-money flows converging on the favorite in a match where historical or qualifying-stage data already tilt heavily toward England. Both sources agree on the direction—England is favored—but diverge on magnitude, making this gap a signal worth monitoring as team news emerges closer to match day.
